template.title: Example template
template.summary: Fork status-plugin-example for a minimal standalone template, or copy plugins/examples/mock-operations from the monorepo.

packageFiles.purpose: Plugin ID, version, permissions, domains, platforms
Native setup fields for account/app configuration
Recorded responses for mapping tests and fixture preview
Plugin documentation published on the website at /plugins/{id}/

workflow.title: Copy the template
workflow.body: Duplicate plugins/examples/mock-operations, rename the folder, and replace example.com with your provider API and fixtures.

workflow.title: Validate locally
workflow.body: Run npm run plugins:validate-local -- path/to/your-plugin from the repository root. Fix schema and cross-file errors before installing.

workflow.title: Test in Developer Mode
workflow.body: Build StatusMac, enable Developer Mode, use Install Local, configure the plugin, run a manual trigger, and preview fixture mappings.

workflow.title: Open a pull request
workflow.body: Include fixtures, declared domains, permissions, and validation evidence. Status reviews, signs, and publishes approved packages to the registry.

hosting.title: Website and docs
hosting.body: Cloudflare Pages serves the marketing site, public documentation, plugin directory, publisher pages, and generated plugin README pages from this repository.

hosting.title: Registry API
hosting.body: A Cloudflare Worker serves plugin metadata, compatibility-filtered versions, registry snapshots, health checks, and revocation data.

hosting.title: Package storage
hosting.body: Cloudflare R2 stores immutable .statusplugin.zip packages, checksums, signatures, manifests, and registry snapshots. Published versions are never overwritten.

governance.title: Canonical source
governance.body: statusfoundry/status is the source of truth for the app, website, registry, schemas, docs, bundled official plugins, examples, and CI.

governance.title: Monorepo first
governance.body: Official plugins start in the main repository so schemas, fixtures, docs, registry metadata, and app-owned views can be reviewed together.

governance.title: Optional splits later
governance.body: Separate status-plugin-{provider} repositories are reserved for large official plugins with independent maintainers or release cycles.

submission.title: No public upload in v1
submission.body: Third-party plugins enter through pull requests, not through a direct upload form. Upload endpoints can exist later as intake, but publication still requires review.

submission.title: Validation first
submission.body: CI validates schemas, declared domains, cross-file references, fixtures, package shape, and checksum output before human review.

submission.title: Reviewed publication
submission.body: Approved packages are signed by Status, uploaded to R2, added to registry metadata, and then installed by the app only after local verification.

trustLevels.body: Built or maintained by Status, open-source by default, signed by Status, and listed in the hosted registry.
Submitted by an external maintainer, reviewed for permissions and behavior, countersigned by Status, and listed after approval.
Installed manually in Developer Mode, unsigned or self-signed, never silently upgraded from the public registry.

downloadFlow: The app reads registry metadata from the active registry Worker URL.
The selected version includes a package URL, SHA-256 hash, signature, permissions, domains, and compatibility metadata.
The app downloads the package from R2-backed storage and verifies hash, signature, compatibility, and revocation locally.
Only after local verification does the app show permissions and install the plugin.

commands.label: Validate plugins changed in a branch

restrictions: No arbitrary native or JavaScript code
No public self-service registry upload in v1
Write actions and elevated permissions require explicit justification
